text: Fill in the blanks about the medulla oblongata.
questions: At the pyramidal decussation, *85/eighty-five* percent of corticospinal fibres cross to the opposite side, forming the *lateral corticospinal* tract.

In Wallenberg syndrome, the artery occluded is the *PICA/posterior inferior cerebellar artery* and the patient shows ipsilateral *Horner/Horner's* syndrome with contralateral loss of pain and temperature.
The nucleus *gracilis* carries fine touch and proprioception from the *lower limb/lower body*, while the nucleus *cuneatus* carries the same modalities from the upper limb.
The *medial lemniscus* is formed by internal arcuate fibres that cross at the *sensory/sensory decussation* level of the medulla.
